intro: Stark and Level Access sit on opposite sides of the transparency line — Stark offers a free tier with paid upgrades (pricing not public), while Level Access only offers custom / contact-sales pricing. Stark is design-tool accessibility plugin for Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD (contrast, vision sim). Level Access is enterprise accessibility compliance program with audits, training, and managed services. Pick Stark when the job is design teams catching a11y issues in figma before engineering and you accept medium lock-in; pick Level Access when it is regulated enterprises needing full-stack a11y platform plus services and you accept medium lock-in. The data we have shows Stark at mostly transparent pricing and docs with average developer experience, and Level Access at opaque pricing and product information with average developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Stark is a poor fit for teams wanting automated scans of production web apps, and Level Access is a poor fit for startups and smbs wanting a lightweight scanner. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature.
quick_take: Stark is for design-tool accessibility plugin; Level Access is for enterprise accessibility compliance program; decide based on whether you can accept sales-call pricing.
verdict_a: Choose Stark if a free tier with room to grow up front matters and the work maps to design-tool accessibility plugin for Figma, Sketch, and Adobe XD (contrast, vision sim).
verdict_b: Choose Level Access if you can work through a partner or sales channel for details and the work lines up with enterprise accessibility compliance program with audits, training, and managed services.

faq.q: Is Stark cheaper than Level Access?
faq.a: Neither Stark nor Level Access publishes entry pricing that lets us compare directly — Stark uses a freemium model and Level Access uses a custom model. Get quotes from both before assuming one is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Stark to Level Access?
faq.a: Migration is possible in either direction. Stark carries medium lock-in and Level Access carries medium lock-in, so plan for meaningful but not blocking rework. Run both in parallel before you fully cut over.

Both Stark and Level Access rate the same on developer experience (3/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Level Access a good alternative to Stark?
faq.a: Yes — Level Access is a reasonable alternative to Stark for design teams catching a11y issues in figma before engineering.. The practical differences are freemium-vs-custom billing and medium-vs-medium lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Level Access as a credible swap.
